# Livery AI Studio — Copilot Instructions

## Project Overview
Livery AI Studio is a desktop application (Flask + pywebview) that uses Google's Gemini AI to generate custom car liveries for iRacing. The frontend is a React + Tailwind CSS single-page app served by a Flask backend.

## Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology |
|-------|-----------|
| Desktop shell | pywebview 5+ |
| Backend | Flask (Python) |
| Frontend | React 19 + Vite 6 |
| Styling | Tailwind CSS v3 (dark theme) |
| Testing | Vitest + React Testing Library |
| AI | Google Gemini API (gemini-3-pro / gemini-3.1-flash) |
| GPU Upscaling | Real-ESRGAN (optional, NVIDIA GPU) |

## Project Structure

```
livery-ai-studio/
├── app.py                  # Flask backend + pywebview window
├── generate_livery.py      # Gemini AI integration
├── deploy_livery.py        # iRacing paint folder deployment
├── extract_psd.py          # Car template PSD extraction (wireframe + diffuse)
├── upscale.py              # Real-ESRGAN GPU upscaling
├── setup.py                # Model download utility
├── patch_basicsr.py        # Torchvision compatibility fix
├── static/                 # Built React app (generated by `npm run build`)
│   ├── index.html          # Built entry point
│   └── assets/             # Bundled JS/CSS
├── frontend/               # React source
│   ├── src/
│   │   ├── components/     # React components
│   │   ├── hooks/          # Custom React hooks
│   │   ├── services/       # API service layer
│   │   ├── utils/          # Utility functions
│   │   └── test/           # Tests
│   ├── vite.config.js      # Vite + Vitest config
│   └── tailwind.config.js  # Tailwind custom theme
├── car_library/            # 180+ pre-extracted car templates
├── legacy/                 # Original vanilla JS/HTML/CSS (reference only)
├── config/                 # Config documentation
├── scripts/                # Utility script documentation
├── requirements.txt        # Python dependencies
└── livery_map.json         # Car template mapping
```

## Architecture: Service → Hook → Component

All backend communication follows this layered pattern:

### 1. Services (`frontend/src/services/`)
Services extend `BaseService` and encapsulate all API calls.

```javascript
// BaseService provides: get(), post(), put(), delete()
import { BaseService } from './BaseService';

class MyService extends BaseService {
  constructor() { super('/api'); }

  async getData() { return this.get('/data'); }
  async saveData(body) { return this.post('/data', body); }
}

export default new MyService(); // singleton
```

**Rules:**
- Every service extends `BaseService`
- Services are exported as singletons
- Services handle raw API communication only — no React state
- Services throw errors on non-OK HTTP responses

**Available services:** `ConfigService`, `SessionService`, `CarsService`, `GenerateService`, `HistoryService`, `UpscaleService`, `LogService`

### 2. Hooks (`frontend/src/hooks/`)
Custom hooks act as the layer between services and components. They manage state and expose simple interfaces.

```javascript
export function useMyFeature() {
  const [data, setData] = useState(null);
  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);

  const load = useCallback(async () => {
    setLoading(true);
    try {
      const result = await myService.getData();
      setData(result);
    } finally {
      setLoading(false);
    }
  }, []);

  return { data, loading, load };
}
```

**Rules:**
- Hooks use services for data fetching — never call `fetch()` directly
- Hooks manage loading/error state
- Hooks expose simple action callbacks to components
- Use `useCallback` for stable function references

**Available hooks:** `useConfig`, `useSession`, `useCars`, `useGenerate`, `useHistory`, `useUpscale`

### 3. Components (`frontend/src/components/`)
Components consume hooks and render UI. They contain no direct API calls.

```javascript
function MyComponent() {
  const { data, loading, load } = useMyFeature();
  // ...
}
```

**Component Organization:**
- `common/` — Reusable UI atoms: `Button`, `Modal`, `StatusBar`, `Toggle`, `FileUploader`
- `layout/` — App shell: `TopBar`, `SubBar`
- `tabs/` — Main content: `GenerateTab`, `HistoryTab`, `UpscaleTab`, `SettingsTab`, `SponsorsTab`, `SpecularTab`
- `modals/` — Dialogs: `SamplePromptsModal`, `SpendingModal`, `HistoryDetailModal`, `CarLibraryModal`, `PromptHistoryModal`, `BrowseUploadsModal`
- `CarPicker.jsx` — Complex car selection dropdown

## Styling Guidelines

### Tailwind Usage
Use Tailwind CSS utility classes. The custom dark theme colors are:

```
bg-bg-dark      #0d0f13   // Main background
bg-bg-panel     #151820   // Top/sub bars
bg-bg-card      #1a1e28   // Cards
bg-bg-input     #1e2230   // Form inputs
bg-bg-hover     #252a38   // Hover states
border-border-default  #2a2f40
text-text-primary   #e8eaf0
text-text-secondary #8890a4
text-text-muted     #555d74
text-accent         #4a6cf7   // Blue
text-success        #34d399   // Green
text-error          #f87171   // Red
text-warning        #fbbf24   // Yellow
```

### Button Variants
Use the `Button` component from `common/Button.jsx`:
- `primary` — Blue action button
- `secondary` — Default bordered button
- `danger` — Red destructive action
- `ghost` — Subtle text button
- `success` — Green confirmation

### Avoid Duplication
- Extract repeated Tailwind patterns into reusable components
- Use `common/` components for UI atoms
- Avoid inline styles; prefer Tailwind utilities

## API Endpoints (Flask)

| Method | Path | Purpose |
|--------|------|---------|
| GET | `/api/config` | Load settings |
| POST | `/api/config` | Save settings |
| GET | `/api/session` | Load form state |
| POST | `/api/session` | Save form state |
| GET | `/api/cars` | List all cars |
| GET | `/api/history` | List generations |
| DELETE | `/api/history/<id>` | Delete generation |
| POST | `/api/generate` | Generate livery |
| POST | `/api/deploy` | Deploy to iRacing |
| POST | `/api/upscale` | GPU upscale |
| GET | `/api/browse-uploads/<category>` | Browse uploads |
| POST | `/api/upload-file` | Upload file |
| GET | `/api/uploads/preview?path=` | Preview file |
| GET | `/api/library/image/<slug>/wire.jpg` | Car wireframe |
| GET | `/api/library/image/<slug>/diffuse.jpg` | Car diffuse |

## Testing

### Running Tests
```bash
cd frontend
npm test           # Run all tests once
npm run test:watch # Watch mode
```

### Writing Tests
- Unit tests for services: mock `fetch` with `vi.fn()`
- Unit tests for utils: pure function testing
- Hook tests: use `renderHook` from `@testing-library/react`
- Component tests: use `render` + `screen` from `@testing-library/react`
- Mock services with `vi.mock('../../services/ServiceName')`

### Test File Location
Tests live in `frontend/src/test/` mirroring the src structure:
```
test/
├── components/     # Component tests (.test.jsx)
├── hooks/          # Hook tests (.test.js)
├── services/       # Service tests (.test.js)
└── utils/          # Utility tests (.test.js)
```

## Development Workflow

### Starting Development
```bash
# Terminal 1: Start Flask backend
python app.py

# Terminal 2: Start Vite dev server (with proxy to Flask)
cd frontend
npm run dev
```

### Building for Production
```bash
cd frontend
npm run build
# This outputs to ../static/ which Flask serves
```

### Adding a New Feature
1. Add a new API endpoint in `app.py`
2. Create/update a service in `frontend/src/services/`
3. Create/update a hook in `frontend/src/hooks/`
4. Create/update a component in `frontend/src/components/`
5. Write tests in `frontend/src/test/`

## Python Backend Guidelines
- All Flask routes use `jsonify()` for JSON responses
- Errors return `{"error": "message"}` with appropriate HTTP status codes
- The `config.json` is always loaded fresh on API calls (no stale config)
- File paths use `pathlib.Path` throughout
- The `car_library/` directory is read-only (source of truth)
- User car overrides go in `data/cars/`

## Git Conventions
- Never commit `config.json` (contains API key)
- Never commit `data/` directory (generated content)
- Never commit `frontend/node_modules/`
- The `static/` directory contains the built React app (committed)
- The `legacy/` directory contains the original vanilla JS app (reference only)

## Naming Conventions
- React components: PascalCase (e.g., `GenerateTab.jsx`)
- Hooks: camelCase starting with `use` (e.g., `useGenerate.js`)
- Services: PascalCase ending in `Service` (e.g., `GenerateService.js`)
- Utils: camelCase (e.g., `pricing.js`, `helpers.js`)
- Tests: same name as file being tested + `.test.js(x)`
